data.title: SNG: Nigeria: UBA to raise $102mln via rights issue to boost capital base
data.description:
Staff Writer

United Bank for Africa Plc (UBA) has announced plans to raise N157 billion through a rights issue.

According to the News Agency of Nigeria (NAN), the disclosure was made via a notice to the Nigerian Exchange Ltd (NGX).

It said in a statement on Thursday that its stock brokers, United Capital Securities limited., had submitted an application to the Nigerian Exchange limited. to that effect.

It added its stock brokers had requested for approval and listing of a Rights Issue of 3,156,869,665 ordinary shares of 50 Kobo each at N50.00 per share

“Trading License Holders are hereby notified that United Bank for Africa Plc, through its Stockbrokers, United Capital Securities Ltd., has submitted an application to the Nigerian Exchange Ltd.” in that regard.

“The rights issue will be on the basis of one new ordinary share for every 13 ordinary shares held as of the close of business on Wednesday, July 16, 2025.

“The qualification date for the Rights Issue is July 16, 2025,” the bank noted.

The move is part of UBA’s strategy to strengthen its capital base and support its expansion and growth objectives across Africa.

data.title: Housing Starts Gain But Still Lag on Year
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

Home-building recovered a little in the U.S. last month but remains down on the year. Here are the main takeaways from the Commerce Department's report released Friday:

  • Housing starts, a gauge of new residential construction, rose to 1.32 million in June from a revised 1.26 million in May, broadly in line with economists' expectations.
  • Despite the monthly gain, starts were 0.5% lower than a year earlier.
  • Residential permits, another measure of trends in housing construction, were little changed on the month at 1.4 million, and similarly remained below the level booked in June last year.
  • President Trump's landmark "big, beautiful bill," which was signed into law earlier this month and offers tax breaks for businesses, could help starts pick up. For now, trade tariffs are sowing uncertainty and pushing up prices in the construction sector. High interest rates are meanwhile weighing on demand, leading to slowdown in the housing market, according to a report from the National Association of Home Builders released earlier this week.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Fed's Waller Says He Would Accept Fed Chair Job If Asked — Market Talk
data.description: Fed governor Christopher Waller has emerged as the leading internal candidate to succeed Jerome Powell as chair, especially in light of his vocal support for interest-rate cuts this year. In a Bloomberg TV interview, Waller doubles down on his arguments for a Fed rate cut at the July 29-30 meeting, and says that though the White House hasn't discussed the chair job with him, he would accept it if offered. "If [Trump] says Chris, I want you to do the job, I would say yes, but he's not talking to me," Waller says. Waller, an academic economist who has spent much of his career in the Fed system, adds that it's important the next chair be someone who has credibility with financial markets. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

data.title: AKBM: MANDATORY NOTIFICATION OF TRADE BY PRIMARY INSIDER
data.description:
July 18, 2025 - Matts Johansen, CEO Aker BioMarine ASA, has today purchased 30000 shares in Aker BioMarine ASA at an average price of NOK 76,61 per sharethrough his wholly-owned investment company KMMN II Invest AS. The shares wereacquired on Oslo Børs. Following the transaction, Johansen holds a total of 1125 837 shares in Aker BioMarine ASA.

This information is considered to be inside information pursuant to the EUMarket Abuse Regulation and is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuantto Section 5-12 the Norwegian Securities Trading Act. This stock exchangeannouncement was published by Lars Jacobsen, General Counsel at Aker BioMarineASA, on 18th July 2025 at 14:55 CEST

About Aker BioMarine:Aker BioMarine is a leading human health and nutrition innovator that developskrill and algae-derived products for consumer health and nutrition. Krill is anatural, powerful, and health-promoting source of nutrients from the pristinewaters of Antarctica, and Aker BioMarine has a unique position in itsindustry.The ingredient portfolio consists of Superba Krill Oil, Lysoveta, FloraMarine,and PL+, as well as the consumer brand, Kori Krill. The innovative approachalsoextends into the spin-offs AION (reduce and recycle plastic waste acrossindustries) and Understory (a protein brand). Aker BioMarine is listed on theOslo Stock Exchange (AKBM). More information is available atwww.akerbiomarine.com.

data.title: Waller's Call for Rate Cut Weighs on Treasury Yields, Dollar — Market Talk
data.description:
0849 ET - Fed Governor Christopher Waller's call for a rate cut in July boosts demands for Treasurys, sending yields down. The dollar also weakens. Waller is seen as a top contender for the central bank's chair. Recent public remarks by Fed officials have mostly cautioned against rate cuts, given the uncertainty created by still fluid tariffs. Odds of a cut in September rise to 58% from 51% yesterday, according to CME data. The 10-year yield is at 4.426% and the two-year at 3.870%. The WSJ Dollar Index falls 0.4%. The greenback weakens 0.5% against both the Swiss franc and the euro. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0845 ET - Six Flags Entertainment's problems are stemming from natural forces more so than business challenges, according to Stifel in a note. Weather trends in 2Q were "horrible," the analysts say, which negatively impacted visitation. The analysts believe those headwinds will make it difficult for Six Flags to hit its full-year guidance, and expect 2Q financial results to be a "full-blown clearing event." Visitation and spending at Six Flags on normal weather days appear to be strong, the analysts add. Stifel believes the market is too focused on near-term results and is underestimating long-term opportunities. Shares are down 39% this year. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

0832 ET - Volvo's end markets remain weak overall, but orders imply it's gaining share, Bank of America Securities analysts write. Second quarter results were solid given its weak end markets, with truck order momentum in both Europe and North America more resilient than implied by recent peer commentary and industry data. "This confirms our view [that] Volvo is likely to gain market share via recent model launches and supply chain improvements." U.S. orders remain weak into the third quarter, but the bank expects order momentum to improve from here, especially in the EU but also in the U.S. if or when tariff rates are eventually settled. The full tariff cost is still to come, but pricing should partly offset this, it says. Shares fall 1.1%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0401 ET - European wholesale banks are expected to report slower trading revenue growth than their U.S. counterparts, Citi says in a research note. This is due to regional and business mix as well as other trends affecting particular banks, the analysts say. The latter include market share losses for Deutsche Bank, negative foreign exchange translation for Barclays and writedown on asset values for UBS, the analysts write. They prefer French banks BNP Paribas and Societe Generale but note that the political risk premium is rising once again for those names. Citi cuts its rating on Barclays to neutral from buy due to its recent rally. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0302 ET - Swiss chemical company Clariant is under significant pressure as it is now involved in a sixth lawsuit regarding anti-competitive practices in the ethylene purchasing market, Vontobel analyst Sibylle Bischofberger writes. The additional lawsuit from U.S.-listed chemical company LyondellBasell is not good for sentiment and no final decision is expected soon, Bischofberger adds. Clariant shares closed Thursday at 8.54 Swiss francs.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Only 16% of Italians would fight for their country, survey shows
data.description:
Almost a third of Italians believe the country will be directly involved in a war within five years, but only 16% of those of fighting age would be willing to take up arms, a survey showed on Friday.

The survey by the Centre for Social Investment Studies (CENSIS) showed 39% of Italians aged between 18 and 45 would declare themselves as pacifist conscientious objectors, 19% would try to evade conscription another way, and 26% would prefer Italy to hire foreign mercenaries.

After years of escalating geopolitical tensions and two ongoing nearby wars, in Ukraine and the Middle East, Italy has joined other NATO nations in pledging to ramp up national defence spending.

However, most Italians remain sceptical of rearmament. Just 26% believe that military deterrence is a good way to ensure peace, while only 25% support increased military budgets even if it means cutting welfare. A mere 11% would like the country to have nuclear weapons.

Despite a 46% rise in defence spending over the past decade, per capita expenditure in Italy remains comparatively low at 586 dollars, the survey said. That compares with 686 dollars in Greece and over 2,000 dollars in countries such as the United States or Norway.

To ensure Italy's security, 49% of all Italians are in favour of strengthening NATO, while 58% would like to see an integrated European Union defence system, with a single army, under a unified command and joint weapons procurement.

Another poll published last month by the European Council of Foreign Relations showed that only 17% of Italians support increasing defence spending, the lowest proportion among 12 European countries surveyed.

data.title: ALK-Abelló's Nasal Spray for Anaphylaxis Wins Approval in UK
data.description:
The UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency on Friday approved ALK-Abelló's (ALK-B.CO) EURneffy adrenaline nasal spray for emergency treatment of anaphylaxis, or severe allergic reactions that are potentially life-threatening.

The marketing authorization allows the use of the single-dose nasal spray in adults and children weighing at least 30 kilograms, providing a needle-free option for patients.

The regulator said it will keep the nasal spray's safety and effectiveness under close review.

Corporacion America Airports' Passenger Traffic Rises 11.3% in June
data.description:
Corporacion America Airports said Friday that June passenger traffic rose 11.3% year over year to 6.9 million.

Total aircraft movements for the month increased 9.2% to 71,400 year over year, while cargo volume grew 2.7% to 31,300 tons, the company said.

data.title: Trump Media Launches 'Truth Social' App for iPads
data.description:
Trump Media & Technology said Friday its 'Truth Social' social media app is now available customized for Apple iPad devices.

The company said the app is designed specifically for the iPad operating system in order to optimize the platform's layout on iPad screens and to enhance cross-platform interaction between the Truth Social and Truth+ platforms.

data.title: Schwab's stock approaches all-time high as 1 million new brokerage accounts boost profit
data.description:
By Steve Gelsi

Trading revenue increases 23% as clients stepped up their activity during April's 'liberation day' selloff

Charles Schwab Corp.'s stock rose in early trading on Friday, and briefly peeked into record territory, after the brokerage and money manager rode a surge in trading to a stronger-than-expected second-quarter profit and revenue.

Schwab (SCHW) said its profit for the three months ending June 30 rose to $1.98 billion, or $1.08 a share, from $4.69 billion, or $5.85 a share, in the year-ago quarter.

Its adjusted profit of $1.14 a share beat the FactSet consensus estimate of $1.10 a share.

Revenue increased to a record $5.85 billion from $4.69 billion, ahead of the analyst estimate of $5.73 billion.

Schwab's stock rose as much as 3.4% in premarket trading after the results were reported, to briefly trade above its record close of $95.53, reached Jan. 14, 2022, before pulling back to be up 2.3%. The stock would have to close up more than 2.5% on Friday to set a new record.

Among the highlights in the quarter, Schwab reported a 23% increase in trading revenue to $952 million from $777 million, as investors scrambled during the steep "liberation day" stock-market selloff in April.

Schwab said it opened more than 1 million new brokerage accounts during the quarter and gathered $80.3 billion in core new assets, up 31%.

Total client assets set a record of $10.76 trillion, up 14% year-over-year.

Also read: Bank stocks see broad rally as U.S.-China trade talks reduce recession predictions.

Schwab's stock has rallied 25.8% in 2025 through Thursday, while Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF XLF has advanced 8.7% and the S&P 500 index SPX has gained 7.1%.

-Steve Gelsi

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
